crisis.label: Elevated
crisis.detail: Jun 30: Iran blocks Oman/UN southern route reopening; attacks Singaporean ship Jun 25; central route mined/closed; Iran insists sole control; IAEA dispute ongoing; France/UK naval taskforce ready; tail 15%

topAlert: JUN 30 IRAN BLOCKS HORMUZ REOPEN: Central route mined; Iran attacks Singaporean ship on southern route; IMO abandons Omani plan; France/UK taskforce ready; Brent $73.68; tail 15%
commodityScenario: Jun 29-30: Iran actively blocking Hormuz reopening. Central route closed by mines. Oman/UN IMO southern route proposal abandoned after Iran attacked a Singaporean tanker using it on Jun 25. Iran FM Araghchi insists only Tehran can decide reopening. MOU (Jun 18) requires only Iran's 'best endeavours' — weak obligation. Limited northern-route traffic under Iranian control remains. France/UK naval taskforce ready to police any agreement. Brent $73.68 (Yahoo Finance Jun 30, -2.1% from Jun 25 $75.04; -27% from D93 peak). Goldman Q4 $80 Brent — contingent on Hormuz reopening timeline. Ras Laffan LNG restart not before late Aug 2026. Tail raised to 15% on ship attack escalation risk.
marketContext: Iran blocking Hormuz reopening; central route mined; southern route abandoned after ship attack (Jun 25); Iran insists sole control; France/UK taskforce ready; Brent $73.68 (Yahoo Finance Jun 30); Goldman Q4 $80; IAEA access dispute; tail 15%.

todaysEvents.summary: Iran blocks Oman/UN southern Hormuz route
todaysEvents.delta: Up: Guardian analysis (Jun 29) reveals Iran rejected a southern route through Omani waters developed with the UN IMO as an alternative to the mined central channel. Iran attacked a Singaporean tanker using the route on Jun 25 (Thu), forcing the IMO to abandon the plan. Iran FM Araghchi in Baghdad: 'Any attempt to adopt new or separate arrangements will only lead to further complications, delays in reopening.' Iran insists it alone controls the strait under maximalist interpretation of the Jun 18 MOU, which only requires 'best endeavours' to lift the blockade. Central route remains closed by mines; limited northern route traffic under Iranian control.

Neutral: Brent $73.68 (Yahoo Finance Jun 30, +0.7% from Mon $73.15). Jun 26 crash to $71.99 (-4.3%) partially reversed. Markets pricing supply normalisation but Guardian reporting shows reopening deeply contested — Iran blocking all routes it doesn't control. WTI ~$70.44. Gold $3,981.80. Goldman Q4 $80 Brent forecast contingent on Hormuz timeline. Risk that markets are pricing too optimistic a reopening given Iran's obstruction. iTraxx Asia IG ~122bp est.; ASEAN HY ~450bp est.

todaysEvents.summary: France/UK naval taskforce ready; Oman legal plan
todaysEvents.delta: Neutral: Guardian reports Macron and Starmer have a naval taskforce ready to police any freedom-of-navigation agreement. Oman's Sultan met Macron on Jun 29 to argue Omani strait management plan — UK commercial legal advice drafted. Omani FM Badr al-Busaidi: 'not in favour of imposing tolls — prohibited internationally — whereas service fees are legal.' France/UK intervention would only deploy if Iran blocks all diplomatic reopening. Thousands of trapped sailors remain; humanitarian operation stalled by route disputes. Iran deputy FM Gharibabadi held joint talks in Muscat — tacit acknowledgment Iran doesn't have sole decision-making power. Doha talks upcoming.

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: Iran eventually accepts an internationally-monitored reopening under combined pressure (Doha talks, Oman plan, France/UK naval threat). Central channel mine-clearing resumes under IMO supervision; southern route reopened with security guarantees. Brent normalises $68-78/bbl on phased supply resumption (Goldman Q4 $80). Ras Laffan restart late Aug 2026. Probability lowered to 60% from 75% after Iran blocked southern route and attacked a ship — reopening clearly harder than markets price.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: Prolonged Iranian obstruction. Doha talks stall on Iran's insistence on sole control; Oman plan fails to gain Iranian consent. Northern-route-only traffic continues at low volume. Iran-IAEA sequencing dispute stalls nuclear track. Brent stays $72-88/bbl. iTraxx Asia IG 128-145bp; JKM holds $16-20/MMBtu. Ras Laffan slips to Q4 2026/Q1 2027. France/UK taskforce deploys to enforce freedom of navigation — naval skirmish risk.

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: Deal collapse. Singaporean tanker attack escalates: US/UK retaliatory strikes or Iran seizes more vessels. MOU voided; Hormuz re-militarised; Brent spikes $110-130/bbl. Probability raised to 15% from 8% (D116) on Jun 25 ship attack signalling IRGC hardliner rejection of MOU terms. Key triggers: second vessel attack, IDF unilateral strike, US Congress sanctions relief veto, Iran walks on frozen-assets impasse.

volatilityDay.note: Tue - Brent $73.68 (+0.7% from Mon). Guardian reveals Iran rejected Oman/UN route, attacked Singaporean tanker Jun 25, IMO abandoned plan. Central route mined; Iran insists sole control. France/UK naval taskforce ready. Markets pricing normalisation but reopening deeply contested. Tail raised 15%.

priceNarratives.Brent: Pre-shock ~$65 → $126 wartime high intraday (Day 59, Apr 30, CNBC/CNN) → $98.57 (Jun 3 D93 peak; Qeshm strike + Kuwait airport + Rubio rejection) → $75.04 (Jun 25, TradingEconomics) → $71.99 (Jun 26, Hormuz reopening hopes crash, Yahoo Finance) → $73.68 (Jun 30, +0.7% from Mon). Iran blocking all routes it doesn't control — Jun 25 ship attack, IMO abandons Omani route. France/UK naval taskforce ready. Markets pricing normalisation but reopening deeply contested. Goldman Q4 $80 contingent on timeline. Working range $70-85 base; $72-88 stress; $110-130 tail.
priceNarratives.JKM LNG: Baseline $9.5 → $23.40/MMBtu (Day 20, Reuters/Platts) → $18.86/MMBtu (last confirmed Jun 12, TradingEconomics; carried). Ras Laffan restart not before late Aug 2026 even on deal; contested Hormuz reopening means elevated prices persist longer than market expects.
priceNarratives.TTF Gas: Pre-shock ~$34/MWh → €49.97/MWh (Day 33) → €49.50/MWh (Jun 3 D93 peak) → €41.54/MWh est. (Jun 26; carried Jun 30; contested Hormuz reopening delays crisis premium unwinding)
priceNarratives.Credit: iTraxx Asia IG est. ~122bp (Jun 30, -73bp from D93 195bp peak; -6bp from D116 128bp); ASEAN HY est. ~450bp (-130bp from D93 580bp; -10bp from D116 460bp); markets still pricing progressive normalisation but Guardian reporting (Iran blocking routes, ship attack) raises re-escalation risk. Tail 15% (+7pp from D116). Wait for Doha talks outcome before removing further CDS protection.
